title: Generative Models for Improved Naturalness, Intelligibility, and Voicing of Whispered Speech

round-A_JuniorNano_output.reasoning:
此為完整長篇論文，聚焦生成式音訊模型在 whispered-to-normal speech 的轉換任務，輸出為音頻波形/特徵，符合納入標準，因此評為5。
關鍵證據：
- 研究標題與摘要明確提出使用生成模型（MelGAN、VQ-VAE）將 whispered speech 轉換為 normal speech，顯示核心關注點在生成式音訊模型與其輸出域。 
- 於方法部分提出 SC-MelGAN 與 SC-VQ-VAE 兩個變體，均為生成式架構並直接或間接輸出語音訊號。 
- 論文內容涵蓋波形/聲碼器層面的處理與端到端轉換能力，且包含 WaveGlow 及 MelGAN 等生成元件，明確聚焦音頻輸出。 
- 使用 wTIMIT 數據集進行訓練與評估，屬於語音信號處理的音頻領域研究。 
- 評估包含客觀指標（RMSE、MCD、Pearson 相關）與主觀 CMOS 測試，並提出以 wav2vec 2.0 embeddings 進行的新型評估方法，顯示以音頻產出與語音特徵為核心。 
- 與 DiscoGAN 作為對比基準，強調生成式音訊模型在轉換任務中的優劣比較。 
- 整體為完整論文結構（導言、方法、實驗、討論、結論），非擴展摘要或其他非完整形式。 
- 其輸出與研究重點皆指向音頻域的合成與轉換，未出現非音頻主輸出之情況。 
結論性證據缺口：目前文本未顯示與音頻以外領域的主要輸出，亦未見非音頻主題的討論，因此不存在明顯的排除或模糊區間。

Evidence: (1) It presents GenerSpeech, a non-autoregressive TTS model for zero-shot style transfer in out-of-domain voices, with detailed architecture (multi-level style adaptor, global representations via wav2vec 2.0, local frame/phoneme/word encoders, Mix-Style Layer Normalization, style-to-content alignment, and a flow-based post-net). Exclusion criteria are not met (no non-audio outputs, no video, no ASR, etc.). Therefore, the paper satisfies the inclusion criteria and is strongly eligible for inclusion. Brief take: This is a full-length, audio-focused generative-model paper with comprehensive methodology and evaluations, warranting inclusion.

title: Meta-StyleSpeech : Multi-Speaker Adaptive Text-to-Speech Generation

round-A_JuniorNano_output.reasoning: This is a full-length, audio-focused generative-model paper (multi-speaker TTS with one-shot adaptation) featuring explicit architecture (StyleSpeech/Meta-StyleSpeech), training strategies, and audio-based evaluations. Evidence: - The paper proposes StyleSpeech, described as a high-quality and expressive multi-speaker adaptive text-to-speech generation model. - SALN, the Style-Adaptive Layer Normalization, adapts gain and bias based on the style vector to generate diverse voices. - The mel-style encoder extracts a style vector from a reference speech X, enabling adaptation from a short audio sample. - Meta-StyleSpeech extends with two discriminators (style and phoneme) and episodic meta-learning to improve adaptation to unseen speakers. - Datasets LibriTTS and VCTK are used; evaluation metrics MOS, MCD, WER, SMOS, Sim; results show improvements and adaptability for both seen and unseen speakers. - The output domain is audio (mel-spectrograms, speech), with no indication of non-audio outputs. - The paper includes experiments on unseen speaker adaptation with varying reference speech lengths and reports quantitative improvements.
